title: 10 best herbal tinctures for acute bronchitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb, Echinacea purpurea, has been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues by reducing inflammation in the airways and fighting off bacterial and viral infections that can cause bronchitis. By taking a dose of purple coneflower tincture, individuals with acute bronchitis may experience relief from symptoms such as coughing, congestion, and wheezing, allowing for faster recovery and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, relieving symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Antimicrobial properties: The tincture's antimicrobial activity can help combat bacterial or viral infections that cause acute bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
Expectorant action: Purple coneflower's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve respiratory congestion.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: A flavonoid glycoside in Echinacea, icariin has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.

Cichoric acid: A caffeic acid derivative found in Echinacea, cichoric acid has been demonstrated to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Alkylamides (e.g., cis-1-alkenyl-3-isobutyl-1-azaspiranes): These alkaloid compounds in Echinacea have been shown to possess immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defenses and potentially helping to reduce the severity and duration of acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high levels of echinacosides, which are believed to have immunomodulatory effects that help alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Leaves: They contain volatile oils and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate coughs associated with acute bronchitis.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids, alkylamides, and other compounds that are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for soothing respiratory tract issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of echinacea purpurea roots and 2 parts of flowers on a dry sunny day.
Dry the plant material in a single layer at 25-30 degrees celsius for 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part of dried plant material with 2 parts of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and mucus production in the airways. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to suppress the replication of respiratory viruses, reducing the severity of symptoms such as coughing and congestion. Additionally, licorice tinctures can soothe irritated mucous membranes, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus, providing relief from acute bronchitis symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular choice for acute bronchitis treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis.
Expectorant property: Licorice tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.

Antiviral property: The saponins present in licorice root have been shown to have antiviral activity against certain viruses that can cause acute bronchitis, such as respiratory syncytial virus (RSV).
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpene saponin helps by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis such as coughing and congestion.

Licoricidin: A phenolic compound, licoricidin exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which may help in reducing the severity and duration of acute bronchitis by modulating the body's immune response.

Liquiritigenin: A flavonoid compound, liquiritigenin has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in protecting the respiratory tract from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: They are used for their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with acute bronchitis.
Barks: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to soothe the respiratory tract and facilitate the clearance of mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra that have been dried for at least 2 weeks.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better solvent penetration.
Mix the chopped roots with 500ml of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for proper extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and glycosides, work to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for mucus to drain out, alleviating congestion and coughing. Additionally, eyebright's expectorant properties help loosen and clear excess mucus, allowing for easier breathing and quicker recovery from acute bronchitis. Its natural and non-invasive approach makes it a popular remedy for soothing irritated airways and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, alleviating congestion and making breathing easier.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in eyebright tincture loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Antispasmodic properties: Eyebright's antispasmodic compounds help relax and calm the bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and coughing fits that can occur during acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with acute bronchitis.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections and reduce oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation, combat infections, and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis, such as coughing and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Euphrasia officinalis leaves are used to make tinctures for acute bronchitis due to their high content of bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Flowers: Euphrasia officinalis flowers are used to make tinctures for acute bronchitis due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and cough.

Roots: Euphrasia officinalis roots are used to make tinctures for acute bronchitis due to their antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which help to fight off underlying infections and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated for 300g.
Clean and chop the harvested euphrasia officinalis plant material into small pieces for 30 minutes.
Combine the chopped euphrasia officinalis with 70% ethanol in a 1:3 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar with the euphrasia officinalis and ethanol mixture daily for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol content in the herb has been shown to help combat bacterial infections, reducing inflammation and congestion in the lungs and airways. By dissolving the tincture under the tongue or mixing it with water, the active compounds are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and expel, which can relieve congestion and coughing.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, help combat bacterial infections that can cause acute bronchitis by killing off the underlying pathogens and preventing their spread.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, like borneol, can help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, which can alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic monoterpene that acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the bacterial load in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol, another phenolic monoterpene, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate is a terpene that has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of thymol, a natural antiseptic and expectorant that helps to relieve bronchitis symptoms.
Stems: Utilized for their antimicrobial properties, which aid in fighting off infections and reducing inflammation associated with acute bronchitis.
Buds: Employed for their ability to stimulate the immune system and promote the expulsion of mucus, providing relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part fresh leaves and flowers of thymus vulgaris in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently swishing it in a bowl of water to remove impurities.
Combine the 1 part plant material with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 50 1 tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 6 weeks shaking the jar every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mistletoe tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as viscotoxins and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, ease congestion, and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, mistletoe tinctures' antimicrobial effects help combat the underlying infection, promoting a faster recovery from acute bronchitis symptoms like coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Mistletoe's tannins and flavonoids have a calming effect on the respiratory tract, helping to soothe and reduce coughing fits associated with bronchitis.
Expectorant properties: The saponins in mistletoe help loosen and expel mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and clear out congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Mistletoe's flavonoids and polyphenols have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the bronchial tubes and lungs, alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Ligustrazine: It helps to relax the airway muscles and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe in patients with acute bronchitis.
Viscumin (Viscum albumin): As an alkaloid, it has immunomodulatory effects, which can help to stimulate the immune system to fight off infections causing acute bronchitis.
Iridoid glucosides (e.g. leucosceptrose): They have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties to help ease bronchitis symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Viscum album contain a higher concentration of saponins and other bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for tinctures.
Buds: The buds of the plant are believed to have a more concentrated effect, as they contain a higher amount of saponins and other medicinal compounds compared to other parts of the plant.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh viscum album at the end of winter or early spring when the sap is rising slowly.
Chop the herb into small pieces using a sharp knife to release the essential oils and resin.
Combine the chopped herb with 500ml of high-proof vodka or a neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the extraction process.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it possesses potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive compounds in elder flowers, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the tincture's natural mucolytic agents help break down mucus, allowing for efficient clearance and relief from coughing fits and wheezing.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Elder tincture's antiviral properties help combat the underlying viral infection causing acute bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Expectorant action: Elder tincture's expectorant properties loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and chest tightness.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the airways and lungs, which can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Isothiocyanates (ITCs): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the infection causing acute bronchitis by inhibiting the growth of pathogens such as influenza and respiratory syncytial virus (RSV).
Flavonoids (such as Quercetin): As powerful antioxidants, flavonoids help reduce inflammation and combat oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.

Triterpenes (such as Ursolic acid): Triterpenes have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help reduce congestion and ease coughing in acute bronchitis, promoting easier mucus expulsion from the lungs.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain a high concentration of sambunigrin, which is converted to sambucine during the drying process, making it effective against acute bronchitis.
Leaves: They possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Fruits: Also known as elderberries, they have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat the underlying viral infection causing acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in quantity of 50% of total volume to be infused.
Combine 100g of sambucus nigra plant material with 500ml of 35% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the container in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to glass bottles and store them in the dark at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The tannins present in yarrow extract help to reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and relief from coughing. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties facilitate the removal of excess mucus, further alleviating symptoms of bronchitis. By targeting the root causes of acute bronchitis, herbal yarrow tinctures provide a natural and effective means of managing this respiratory condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as achillein and sesquiterpenes, help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate respiratory distress, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in yarrow tincture can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, bronchi, and throat, reducing congestion and coughing.

Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses that cause respiratory infections, including acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Achillin: Achillin is a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.

Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid is a phenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways, eliminate infection, and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, making them effective in soothing respiratory issues.
Flowers: High in essential oils and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease bronchial spasms.
Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm bronchial congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in abundance at the peak of their potency in summer.
Dry the harvested plant material thoroughly in a warm place for at least 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried achillea millefolium with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it daily for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to yield the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant's flavonoids, taraxasterol, and sesquiterpenes help to reduce inflammation in the airways, soothing irritated tissues and relaxing constricted bronchi. Additionally, dandelion's natural mucilage content coats and lubricates the throat and respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and promote healthy drainage. By alleviating congestion and reducing inflammation, herbal dandelion tinctures can provide quick relief from acute bronchitis symptoms, allowing for a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can ease congestion and coughing associated with acute bronchitis.

Expectorant properties: The bitter constituents of dandelion tincture, like sesquiterpene lactones, stimulate the production of mucus and facilitate its clearance from the lungs, making it easier to expel excess phlegm and relieve congestion.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content helps protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bronchial inflammation and scarring during acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with acute bronchitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and congestion in the airways.
Taraxasterol: This triterpene saponin has been shown to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to thin mucus and reduce coughing associated with acute bronchitis.
Polyphenols: These antioxidants help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by respiratory viruses, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Roots: Roots are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and irritation in the bronchial tubes.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help ease coughing and reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in late spring or early summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Wash the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with cold water to remove any impurities or debris.
Chop the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine 250 grams of chopped taraxacum officinale with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the inflammation in the airways, allowing for easier breathing. The herb's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and expel. Additionally, yellow jessamine has natural antiviral and antibacterial compounds that can help to combat the underlying infection causing the bronchitis, reducing its severity and duration.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Yellow Jessamine helps to relax and calm down the spasms in the airways, which alleviates coughing fits and makes breathing easier.

Expectorant properties: The herb increases mucus production and helps to loosen and eliminate excess mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, thereby relieving congestion and bronchial obstruction.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Yellow Jessamine's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to chronic bronchitis and respiratory damage.
remedy_constituents: Gelsemine: It is an indole alkaloid that helps alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
Gelseminine: It is a related alkaloid that also helps with acute bronchitis by exhibiting bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve congestion and ease breathing.

Flavonoids: Specifically, kaempferol, a type of flavonoid found in Gelsemium sempervirens, may help with acute bronchitis by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Gelsemium sempervirens are the most commonly used part in making tinctures for acute bronchitis due to their high content of alkaloids, particularly gelsemine.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make tinctures for acute bronchitis as they contain similar alkaloids to the roots, providing similar therapeutic effects.
Barks: The barks are used to make tinctures for acute bronchitis as they contain gelsemine and other alkaloids that help in alleviating respiratory symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh leaves and stems of gelsemium sempervirens in the morning.
Chop the material into small pieces and combine with 500 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol.
Store the mixture in a glass jar and allow it to steep in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dodder tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant's constituents, such as flavonoids and lignans, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, dodder tincture's mucilaginous properties help to soothe and calm the bronchi, allowing for smoother passage of mucus and easier elimination of trapped pathogens. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to alleviate congestion, coughing, and chest tightness associated with acute bronchitis, promoting faster recovery and reduced symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dodder tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which helps to alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.

Expectorant properties: Dodder tincture can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieving congestion and chest tightness associated with bronchitis.

Antiviral properties: Dodder tincture has been found to have antiviral activity against certain viruses that cause acute bronchitis, such as influenza A and B, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Cuscuta epithymum tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Cuscuta epithymum tinctures exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by acute bronchitis.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Cuscuta epithymum tinctures have expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Stems: Stems of Cuscuta species are used to make tinctures due to their reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis.
Leaves: Leaves of Cuscuta species are used in herbal remedies for their potential antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can aid in the treatment of acute bronchitis.
Roots: Roots of Cuscuta species are used in traditional medicine for their reported expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and cough associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cuscuta epithymum roots and leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant surface.
Dry the harvested cuscuta epithymum roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried cuscuta epithymum with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days with occasional gentle shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth and bottle it in a dark glass container with a tight-fitting lid for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The bioactive compounds in fennel, such as anethole and fenchol, have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the lungs and bronchi. Additionally, fennel's antispasmodic properties can relax bronchial muscles, allowing for easier breathing and relief from wheezing and coughing. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from acute bronchitis symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and improved respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
Antispasmodic property: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds help relax the smooth muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing coughing spasms and relieving bronchial constriction.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the airways, which can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving cough and congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
Seeds: Containing a volatile oil that helps to open up airways and ease respiratory congestion, making them a natural remedy for bronchial spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers on a sunny day to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Chop 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare into small pieces and pack them tightly into a clean glass jar.
Combine one part chopped foeniculum vulgare with one part vodka in the jar, sealing it tightly and allowing it to steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile have been shown to relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate coughing fits and reduce anxiety, allowing individuals to rest more comfortably and recover faster from the illness. By promoting relaxation and relieving respiratory symptoms, herbal chamomile tinctures offer natural relief for those suffering from acute bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, relieving congestion and coughing.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in chamomile, like matricine, relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce spasms, making it easier to breathe and alleviating wheezing and coughing fits.

Sedative properties: Chamomile's sedative compounds, such as apigenin, calm the nervous system and help regulate respiratory function, reducing stress and anxiety associated with acute bronchitis and promoting a restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, making it effective in alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Flowers: Containing essential oils such as alpha-bisabolol, which has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the respiratory tract during an acute bronchitis episode.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for tincture production at dawn.
Combine one part of the harvested material with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 14 days and 14 nights without shaking or disturbing.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Store the final tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it has been traditionally used to combat respiratory infections. The active ingredients in umckaloabo, such as isomaltose and arabinose, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion in the lungs. This natural remedy also stimulates mucociliary clearance, which aids in the removal of excess mucus and debris from the airways, promoting a faster recovery from bronchitis symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Umckaloabo tincture contains mucilages, which soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acute bronchitis.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in Umckaloabo tincture help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate lung damage and reduce the risk of complications from acute bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Umckaloabo tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as harpagoside, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making breathing easier and more comfortable.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and relax bronchial muscles.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe bronchial tubes and ease congestion, making them effective for treating acute bronchitis.
Bark: The bark is rich in tannins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and combat respiratory infections, effectively alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality pelargonium sidoides roots in late summer or early fall.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sassafras tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and open airways. The expectorant properties in sassafras help to loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out the lungs. Additionally, sassafras's anti-inflammatory compounds can soothe irritated tissues and calm the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and discomfort. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from acute bronchitis symptoms, such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath, allowing for a faster recovery and reduced risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Sassafras tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing.
Anti-inflammatory: Sassafras contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms such as chest tightness and wheezing.
Antibacterial: Sassafras tincture has antibacterial properties that can help combat the underlying infection causing bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
Barks: The barks contain a compound called safrole, which has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and remove mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sassafras albidum root bark and cut it into small pieces for drying.
Dry the sassafras albidum root bark in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crisp.
Combine 250 grams of dried sassafras albidum root bark with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the tincture to infuse.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 2 hours of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rhubarb tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation in the airways. The tannins present in rhubarb help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby relieving congestion and coughing. Additionally, rhubarb's expectorant properties promote the clearance of excess mucus from the lungs, allowing for improved airflow and reducing symptoms associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Rhubarb tincture's antitussive property helps to relieve coughing fits and reduce mucus production, providing relief from acute bronchitis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rhubarb tincture's anti-inflammatory property reduces inflammation in the airways, which can help to ease congestion, wheezing, and chest tightness associated with acute bronchitis.

Expectorant property: Rhubarb tincture's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reduce symptoms such as congestion and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which have been shown to help relieve bronchial spasms and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Rhyzomes: Contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that help to expel mucus and ease congestion in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhubarb roots in the early morning dew when they are at their highest potency.
Clean and dry the harvested roots using a gentle stream of water and soft cloth paper towels.
Chop the dried rhubarb roots into small pieces and mix with 50-60% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to allow for proper infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to amber glass bottles for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating congestion and coughing. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of bronchial symptoms. By using herbal ginger tinctures, individuals with acute bronchitis may experience relief from their symptoms and speed up their recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant properties: Ginger has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving coughing and chest congestion associated with bronchitis.
Antiviral properties: Ginger's antimicrobial compounds may help combat viral infections that cause acute bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing cough and relieving respiratory congestion.
Roots: Ginger roots are rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm the bronchial passages, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots at dawn and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces weighing exactly 200g and combine with 500ml of vodka.
Steep the ginger root mixture in a dark glass jar for 14 days, shaking daily at dawn and dusk.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean dark glass bottle discarding solids.
Store the final zingiber officinale tincture in an amber-colored glass bottle with tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild garlic tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the airways and ease congestion. The expectorant properties of wild garlic also help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits. Additionally, wild garlic has antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections that may be causing the bronchitis, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Wild garlic tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion. This is because wild garlic contains compounds like allyl sulfide, which helps to break down thick mucus and reduce its viscosity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild garlic tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to bronchitis symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.

Antiviral properties: Wild garlic tincture contains compounds like ajoene, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause acute bronchitis. This helps to reduce the severity and duration of the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild garlic are rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm respiratory issues such as bronchitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of wild garlic contain expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh allium ursinum bulbs and clean them thoroughly with distilled water removing any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned bulbs into small pieces weighing approximately 100g for each 500ml of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol.
Combine the chopped bulb pieces with 500ml of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar filling it to the top.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the liquid to infuse and extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because they soothe and calm inflamed airways, reducing congestion and discomfort. The mucilage in marshmallow root creates a protective film that shields the respiratory tract from further irritation, allowing the body to heal naturally. Additionally, marshmallow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease coughing fits, making it easier to breathe and recover from acute bronchitis. As a natural expectorant, it also helps loosen mucus, promoting a faster clearing of infection.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tincture contains a high concentration of mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acute bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tincture can help reduce swelling and constriction in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
Expectorant actions: Marshmallow tincture has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of mucilage, which helps soothe and protect irritated bronchial tissues, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Barks: The barks contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow roots althaea officinalis of good quality weighing around one kilogram.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and air dry them in a shaded area for seven days.
Transfer the dried root pieces to a clean glass jar and cover with 95 proof vodka filling container three-quarters.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks shaking daily during this period to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The mucilage in plantain soothes and protects the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bronchial congestion. Additionally, plantain's expectorant action helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving coughing fits. By addressing both inflammation and mucus buildup, plantain tinctures can provide effective relief for acute bronchitis symptoms, allowing for a faster recovery and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and shortness of breath.
Expectorant: Plantain's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieving congestion.
Antiviral: Plantain tincture's antiviral properties help combat viral infections that cause bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the plantain plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, making them effective in treating acute bronchitis.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of plantain have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues, including acute bronchitis, by reducing coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves at dawn on a dry day when they are at their peak potency.
Wash the harvested leaves gently in cold running water and pat them dry with a clean cloth immediately.
Chop 100g of fresh plantago major leaves into small pieces and transfer to a clean glass jar.
Pour 300ml of vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped plant material in the jar and seal it tightly.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds fully.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for acute bronchitis are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery from bronchitis. They work by soothing the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system. The best herbal tinctures for acute bronchitis include Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Sambucus nigra, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Verbascum thapsus, Hydrastis canadensis, Echinacea angustifolia, and Ephedra sinica. If left untreated, acute bronchitis can lead to respiratory failure, prolonged recovery, and decreased quality of life, causing significant distress and impacting daily activities, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acute bronchitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are coughs and respiratory issues such as asthma, chronic bronchitis, and pneumonia. Herbal tinctures like Echinacea, Thyme, and Goldenseal have antiviral and antibacterial properties that help soothe irritated airways, reduce inflammation, and combat infection. They may also be used to treat other conditions like sinusitis, tonsillitis, and laryngitis, which share similar symptoms with acute bronchitis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with acute bronchitis is a blend of Echinacea, Thyme, and Ginger. Echinacea enhances the immune system, while Thyme's antimicrobial properties combat bacterial infections. Ginger helps reduce inflammation, soothes the throat, and eases congestion. A standard dosage is to combine 20-30 drops of each tincture in water, three to four times a day. This synergistic blend can help alleviate symptoms, promote recovery, and reduce the risk of complications from acute bronchitis.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current Medical Research and Opinion, herbal tinctures such as HL301 can be good for acute bronchitis due to their efficacy in reducing symptoms. In the study, HL301, a combination product of seven medicinal plants, was compared to Umckamin in a phase III, randomized, controlled, double-blind, multicenter trial design. The results showed that HL301, given at a dose of 600mg/day for seven days, was as effective as Umckamin, given at a dose of 333mg/day, in treating acute bronchitis, with both treatments showing similar improvements in bronchitis severity scores and safety profiles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it boosts the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of the illness. Its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate congestion and coughing, while its antiviral properties help combat the underlying infection. The tincture's ability to stimulate mucus production and thin it out also makes it easier for the body to expel, providing relief from bronchial irritation and coughing. This natural remedy supports the body's natural healing process, promoting recovery from acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: This flavonoid glycoside helps with acute bronchitis by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged tissues in the respiratory tract.
Cichoric acid: This caffeic acid derivative exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to combat bacterial and viral infections causing acute bronchitis.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation, combat oxidative stress, and promote the recovery of the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: As they contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bronchitis symptoms.
Leaves: With their high content of flavonoids and caffeic acid, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate bronchitis symptoms.
Flowers: As they are rich in alkylamides and other compounds that have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help boost the immune system and reduce bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a clean and organic source in the morning.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator or by air-drying for 7 days.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder with a 1:5 ratio.
Combine the powdered plant material with a 60% vodka solution in a clean glass jar in a 1:2 ratio by volume.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 15 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it contains potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties that soothe and calm the respiratory system. The expectorant properties in Eucalyptus globulus tinctures help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the bronchial tubes, providing fast and effective relief from acute bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to thin and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
Piperitone: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and difficulty breathing.

Caffeic acid: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the respiratory tract from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by acute bronchitis, thereby promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides expectorant and decongestant properties.
Buds: Eucalyptus globulus buds are used to make tinctures due to their high content of volatile oils, which have a calming effect on the respiratory system and help relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and clean them thoroughly with a brush to remove dirt.
Chop the leaves into smaller pieces using a sharp knife to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped leaves with 40% of their weight in 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to obtain the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, the expectorant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and cough up infected secretions, ultimately alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis. This makes it a popular natural remedy for bronchitis relief.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help with acute bronchitis by reducing inflammation in the airways and relieving cough symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the bronchial passages and reduce congestion.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part for making tinctures, as they contain high amounts of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make tinctures, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Barks: The barks are sometimes used to make tinctures, as they contain glycosides and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early fall when the roots are mature and dry.
Chop the roots into small pieces and dry them at 40°c for 2-3 hours to preserve their properties.
Combine 500g of the dried roots with 75% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks with occasional agitation to extract the active compounds from the roots.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it is rich in antiviral and antimicrobial properties that combat the underlying infection. The tincture's high concentration of flavonoids and polyphenols also helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion. Additionally, Sambucus nigra tinctures have been traditionally used to soothe a sore throat and promote a productive cough, aiding in the removal of mucus and debris from the airways. This holistic approach helps to alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to reduce inflammation and improve immune response, aiding in the recovery from acute bronchitis.
Phenolic acids: Possess antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections causing acute bronchitis.
Organic acids (malic and tartaric acids): Act as expectorants, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used to make tinctures for acute bronchitis due to their high content of bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of acute bronchitis symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits, also known as elderberries, are used to make tinctures for their antiviral properties, which can help to combat the underlying viral infection causing acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the ripe black elder berries in the late summer or early fall season when they are fully dark purple in color.
Clean and dry the harvested berries in a dark room with good air circulation for several days to prevent mold.
Crush the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to release their active properties.
Steep the powder in a neutral carrier oil such as grapeseed oil in a ratio of 1 part powder to 2 parts oil for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause bronchitis. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. This can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath, promoting a faster recovery from acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: It has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause acute bronchitis.
Carvacrol: Its expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and recover from bronchitis.
Rosmarinic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are a primary source for thyme tinctures due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound known for its decongestant and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: Thyme stems are rich in essential oils, including thymol, which helps to relieve respiratory issues associated with acute bronchitis.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to create a more delicate and soothing tincture, often combined with other herbs to provide a gentle yet effective remedy for acute bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a dry sunny morning.
Clean and dry the thymus vulgaris material in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a dark glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the expectorant properties of the tincture help to loosen and clear mucus, reducing the severity of bronchitis symptoms. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to alleviate coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, offering a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and promote the removal of mucus, making it easier to breathe.
Shogaols: Shogaols have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the underlying infections causing acute bronchitis, such as bacterial or viral infections.
Gingerol-derived Volatile Oils: These oils have a bronchodilatory effect, helping to relax the airway muscles and improve airflow, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: As the primary part of the plant, roots contain most of the bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which aid in relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Buds: Young ginger buds are often used to make tinctures, as they are rich in volatile oils that help ease congestion and coughs associated with bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned zingiber officinale rhizomes into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped zingiber officinale with 80-proof vodka in a ratio of 1:2 by weight to create a strong tincture.
Place the mixture in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow the infusion to occur.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle to remove the solids and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its mucolytic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms the mucous membranes in the lungs, relieving congestion and coughing. It also reduces inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and recover from the condition. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus has antimicrobial properties, which help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the acute bronchitis. This makes it a valuable natural remedy for acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways associated with acute bronchitis.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds possess bronchodilatory properties, which can help relax the airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with acute bronchitis.
Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections caused by bacteria and viruses that lead to acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they are rich in mucilages, which help soothe and calm inflamed bronchial passages.
Stems: They are used because their antispasmodic properties can help relax and calm the bronchial muscles, relieving coughing and wheezing.
Flowers: They are used because they contain volatile oils and flavonoids with expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the bronchial tubes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves in large quantities when they are in full bloom.
Chop the harvested plant material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 70 ethanol solvent in a ratio of 1:5 by volume in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container to separate the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains berberine, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections in the respiratory tract. This helps alleviate symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, Hydrastis canadensis tinctures may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes, supporting the body's natural healing process and promoting a quicker recovery from acute bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity and duration of acute bronchitis by combating bacterial infections and soothing inflamed airways.
Hydrastine: It exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
Palmitic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate coughing in acute bronchitis, promoting faster recovery and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of hydrastine and berberine, which are responsible for the plant's expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They contain hydrastine and berberine, which help to relieve coughs and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Barks: They contain berberine, which is a key compound that helps to reduce inflammation and fight infections in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:8 or 1:5 ratio of dried roots to solvent in a clean environment.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 70% ethanol or vodka.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because it contains compounds that support the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of the illness. The plant's active ingredients, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause bronchitis. By modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, Echinacea angustifolia tinctures can alleviate symptoms and promote recovery from acute bronchitis, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory infections.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: These constituents help with acute bronchitis by exerting anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which aid in reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response to alleviate bronchitis symptoms.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides found in Echinacea angustifolia tinctures contribute to its therapeutic benefits against acute bronchitis by acting as immunostimulants, enhancing the immune system's ability to fight off infections.

Polysaccharides: The polysaccharides in Echinacea angustifolia tinctures help with acute bronchitis by stimulating the immune system and increasing the production of interferon, which plays a crucial role in defending against viral infections that can cause bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and calm bronchial passages.
Leaves: They contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease respiratory issues, making them useful for treating acute bronchitis.
Roots: (Again, as roots are the primary part used in Echinacea angustifolia tinctures, and I've already mentioned their uses)
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried echinacea angustifolia roots by hand in the fall season after flowering.
Chop 1 part of the harvested roots into smaller pieces using a sharp knife or a root cutter.
Mix 10 parts of 80 proof vodka with 1 part of the chopped roots in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica tinctures helps with acute bronchitis because of its potent expectorant properties, which facilitate the removal of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract. The tincture's ability to dilate airways and increase blood flow to the lungs also aids in reducing inflammation and congestion. Additionally, Ephedra sinica's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the bronchitis. By addressing these underlying issues, Ephedra sinica tinctures can provide relief from acute bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing.
Norephedrine: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Sinicaine (Ephedrine methyl ester): Demonstrates expectorant properties, facilitating the removal of mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which help relieve respiratory symptoms and open up airways.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain ephedrine and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain ephedrine and other compounds that help relax bronchial muscles and promote expectoration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh ephedra sinica stems with leaves in the early morning or late afternoon to minimize moisture content.
Dry the ephedra sinica stems with leaves in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours.
Grind 100g of dried ephedra sinica stems with leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 250ml of 80% ethanol with 100g of ephedra sinica powder in a clean glass jar and steep for 2-4 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.
